name: Breakfast Focaccia
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the thawed dough in a lightly oiled 10x15″ pan. Stretch and press to fill the pan evenly. If the dough is too elastic to stay in place, let it rest for a few minutes, then press. Cover the dough lightly with plastic wrap and let it stand until puffy, about 45 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, pit the plums and cut them into 1/4"-thick slices. Brush the puffy dough with 1 tablespoon of butter. Arrange the plum slices on the dough without overlapping. Brush the fruit with the remaining butter and sprinkle evenly with the sugar-cinnamon mixture.
3. Bake the focaccia on the bottom rack in a 350°F oven until well browned on the edges and bottom (lift gently with a spatula to check), about 40 minutes. Serve warm. If made ahead, cool, wrap airtight, and hold at room temperature until the next day. Reheat, uncovered, in a 350°F oven until warm, 5-10 minutes.
